source_paper.title: Critical Cooling and Warming Rates as a Function of CPA Concentration

extraction_metadata.extraction_notes: This paper compiles CCR and CWR data across many individual CPAs as a function of concentration. It provides the most comprehensive single-source view of how concentration affects vitrification tendency. Key insight: CWR is always higher than CCR (warming is the harder step).

key_relationships.general_rule: CCR and CWR decrease exponentially with increasing CPA concentration
key_relationships.cwr_vs_ccr: CWR is typically 5-100x higher than CCR for the same solution. Warming is the dominant bottleneck.
key_relationships.volume_dependence: CCR/CWR increase with sample volume (harder to cool/warm uniformly at larger scales)
key_relationships.practical_implication: To vitrify large organs, you need solutions with very low CCR/CWR — which means high concentrations — which means more toxicity. This is the fundamental tradeoff.

individual_cpa_data.name: DMSO
individual_cpa_data.notes: DMSO is one of the best single-agent vitrifiers. Strong hydrogen bond disruptor.

individual_cpa_data.name: Ethylene glycol
individual_cpa_data.notes: EG produces the strongest freezing point depression per mole among common CPAs

individual_cpa_data.name: Glycerol
individual_cpa_data.notes: Glycerol is less effective per weight than DMSO but has lower toxicity

individual_cpa_data.name: Propylene glycol
individual_cpa_data.notes: PG is commonly used in 2-component mixtures (e.g., DP6 with DMSO)

individual_cpa_data.name: Formamide
individual_cpa_data.notes: Very effective vitrifier but significantly more toxic than DMSO or glycerol. Used in VS55 and M22.

cocktail_comparison.description: How multi-component cocktails compare to single agents — the key insight is that mixtures achieve much lower CCR/CWR at lower total concentrations than any single agent
cocktail_comparison.insight: M22 achieves CCR 400x lower than DP6, and CWR 470x lower, with only 40% more total solute. The multi-component + ice blocker strategy is dramatically more effective than simply adding more of a single CPA.
